channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Which teams are in the World Cup quarterfinals, and what’s the schedule?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals lineup as it builds, with match kickoff times and locations.

item.title: Bellingham inspires 10-man England to beat Mexico 3-2 in last-16 thriller
item.description: England hands Mexico their first World Cup loss at Estadio Azteca, winning 3-2 to reach the 2026 quarterfinals.

item.title: OPEC+ countries say they will expand monthly oil production
item.description: Seven OPEC+ members, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, to increase output by 188,000 barrels per day.

item.title: Sports stadium becomes home for victims of the Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: A sports stadium in La Guaira state has been turned into a makeshift home and logistics centre for thousands of victims.

item.title: Ronaldo’s last World Cup? We will see, says Portugal star before Spain clash
item.description: 'I will finish when I choose,' Ronaldo reveals amid speculation about his World Cup future ahead of the last-16 match.

item.title: How conflict minerals fuel war in eastern DR Congo amid US sanctions
item.description: The move underscores growing scrutiny of the conflict mineral trade fuelling violence in eastern DR Congo.

item.title: Tuareg fighters claim to have shot down a Russian helicopter in Mali
item.description: Tuareg fighters in Mali have released footage claiming to have shot down a Russian Africa Corps Mi-24 helicopter.

item.title: Neymar quits international football after Brazil’s World Cup knockout loss
item.description: The injury-plagued striker converted a penalty but failed to take his team into the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Belgium ‘astonished’ at FIFA’s U-turn on Balogun red card for USA match
item.description: Belgium's football federation is 'investigating all potential options' to safeguard football after FIFA's U-turn.

item.title: Iran war live: Khamenei’s funeral procession under way in Tehran
item.description: The funeral procession is expected to last 10-12 hours and has drawn crowds of hundreds of thousands of mourners.

item.title: Venezuelan leader marks Independence Day with message of ‘no social unrest’
item.description: Interim President Delcy Rodriguez once again defends her government's handling of the deadly earthquakes on June 24.

item.title: How a son rescued his father from the rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
item.description: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

item.title: Haaland scores twice as Norway stun Brazil 2-1 in World Cup 2026 last 16
item.description: Haaland's brace takes him to seven goals for the tournament as Norway book a quarterfinal against Mexico or England.

item.title: Palestinian baby dies in West Bank after Israel blocks urgent medical care
item.description: Israeli forces also shoot dead a 16-year-old boy in the occupied West Bank and kill two Palestinians in Gaza.

item.title: Turkiye gears up for its first NATO summit in 22 years
item.description: Leaders of the NATO member states are expected in Turkiye by July 7 for a summit - the first it will host in 22 years.

item.title: Albanian court frees protesters who rallied against Kushner-linked resort
item.description: The planned resort linked to US President Donald Trump's son-in-law has become a flashpoint for anti-government protests

item.title: Mexico 2-3 England: FIFA World Cup 2026 last 16 – as it happened
item.description: All the updates from our live text commentary stream coverage as Jude Bellingham leads England into the quarterfinals.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: All to know as Ronaldo’s Portugal play Spain in the last 16
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo is just one of several key talking points before Portugal and Spain clash in a round of 16 epic in Dallas.

item.title: 10,000 people evacuated as wildfire tears through southern France
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire has forced around 10,000 people to evacuate after burning more than 1,500 hectares in France.

item.title: How will the rise of right-wing populism affect Latin America?
item.description: Conservatives are winning elections across the region.

item.title: 8 injured, including 4 children, in New York City Fourth of July shooting
item.description: A shooting at a Fourth of July celebration in the US city of New York wounded eight victims, four of whom are children.

item.title: Volunteers vow to keep searching for Venezuela earthquake survivors
item.description: Rescue teams in Venezuela’s La Guaira State say they’re never going to give up their search for earthquake survivors.

item.title: Australia thrash England to win record seventh Women’s T20 World Cup
item.description: Beth Mooney (64) and Phoebe Litchfield (48) lead Australia’s stunning seven-wicket win over the hosts at Lord’s.

item.title: 12-tonne whale removed from DR Congo beach
item.description: A dead whale weighing about 12 tonnes has been recovered from the beaches of Muanda.

item.title: America at 250: Democracy’s greatest experiment?
item.description: Chris Hedges reflects on 250 years of US independence - the nation's ideals, contradictions, and what it has become.
